message:
docs: align AGENTS.md and ARCHITECTURE.md with current fsqlite storage stack

The project recently migrated its SQLite storage layer from rusqlite
(with bundled modern_sqlite features) to the fsqlite stack, which
consists of three path-dependency crates: fsqlite, fsqlite-types, and
fsqlite-error. However, the developer-facing documentation in AGENTS.md
and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md still referenced the old rusqlite dependency,
which would mislead contributors into looking at the wrong crate when
debugging storage issues or adding new storage features.

This commit updates both documents to accurately describe the current
storage dependency topology:

AGENTS.md:
- Key Dependencies table: replaced "rusqlite" row with
  "fsqlite + fsqlite-types + fsqlite-error" and clarified these are
  path dependencies providing an engine facade plus shared types/errors.
- ASCII architecture diagram: changed "SQLite (rusqlite, bundled)" to
  "SQLite (fsqlite stack)" to match the actual crate wiring.

docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:
- SqliteStorage prose section: updated the description from "using
  rusqlite" to "using the fsqlite stack (fsqlite, fsqlite-types, and
  fsqlite-error)".
- Key Dependencies table: replaced rusqlite with the fsqlite trio,
  consolidated the thiserror + anyhow rows into one, removed the rayon
  row (no longer a direct dependency), and added three new crates that
  are now part of the active dependency set: rich_rust (rich terminal UI
  components), toon_rust (TOON format support), and self_update
  (optional release-based self-update support).

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Added three new issue records tracking this work:
  - beads_rust-1xs5 (closed): quality-gate sweep task that surfaced a
    compile error in the fsqlite stack.
  - beads_rust-21r7 (closed): the documentation alignment task itself.
  - beads_rust-2i88 (blocked, P1): a bug report for the fsqlite
    mutability mismatch build failure (concurrent_rollback_to_savepoint
    receives &ConcurrentHandle but expects &mut ConcurrentHandle),
    which currently blocks cargo check/clippy on the workspace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -59,7 +59,7 @@ We only use **Cargo** in this project, NEVER any other package manager.
 | Crate | Purpose |
 |-------|---------|
 | `clap` | CLI parsing with derive macros + shell completions |
-| `rusqlite` | SQLite storage (bundled, modern_sqlite features) |
+| `fsqlite` + `fsqlite-types` + `fsqlite-error` | SQLite engine facade plus shared storage types/errors (path dependencies) |
 | `serde` + `serde_json` | Issue serialization and JSONL export |
 | `schemars` | JSON Schema generation for robot output |
 | `chrono` | Timestamp parsing and RFC3339 formatting |
@@ -214,7 +214,7 @@ CLI (clap derive)
     ├── Commands ────── 35+ subcommands (create, list, show, close, dep, sync, ...)
     │                       │
     │                       ▼
-    ├── Storage ─────── SQLite (rusqlite, bundled)
+    ├── Storage ─────── SQLite (fsqlite stack)
     │                       │
     │                       ├── Schema (migrations, JSONL ↔ SQLite sync)
     │                       ├── Events (append-only audit log)

@@ -198,7 +198,8 @@ br sync --flush-only
 
 ### SqliteStorage
 
-The primary storage implementation using rusqlite.
+The primary storage implementation using the fsqlite stack (`fsqlite`,
+`fsqlite-types`, and `fsqlite-error`).
 
 ```rust
 pub struct SqliteStorage {
@@ -656,14 +657,15 @@ impl IssueValidator {
 | Crate | Purpose |
 |-------|---------|
 | `clap` | CLI parsing with derive macros |
-| `rusqlite` | SQLite storage (bundled) |
+| `fsqlite` + `fsqlite-types` + `fsqlite-error` | SQLite engine facade plus shared storage types/errors |
 | `serde` + `serde_json` | Serialization |
 | `chrono` | Timestamps |
 | `sha2` | Content hashing |
-| `thiserror` | Error types |
-| `anyhow` | Error context |
+| `thiserror` + `anyhow` | Error types and context |
 | `tracing` | Structured logging |
-| `rayon` | Parallel processing |
+| `rich_rust` | Rich terminal UI components |
+| `toon_rust` | TOON format support |
+| `self_update` (optional) | Release-based self-update support |
 
 ---
